recommendations: Add a minimal but real test suite for the MCP wrapper (unit tests for each exposed endpoint plus a few end-to-end sentiment scoring cases)
Remove or hide internal/private VADER helpers from public MCP endpoints (especially underscored functions) and expose a smaller stable API centered on `SentimentIntensityAnalyzer`
Add strict input/output schemas with Pydantic models (clear typing for text input
Implement robust error handling and validation (empty text
Create CI pipelines (lint + tests + basic smoke run of `start_mcp.py` on push/PR)
and avoid repeated resource loads per request)
Harden security and runtime limits (input size caps
and safe defaults to prevent abuse)
Improve README_MCP with concrete usage examples (sample MCP calls
Add compatibility tests across Python versions supported by VADER/MCP dependencies
Separate generated plugin code from source with clearer module boundaries and docstrings for maintainability
Add contract tests to ensure adapter-mode import behavior remains stable if upstream `vaderSentiment.py` changes
Add a fallback strategy when import fails (graceful degraded mode and actionable startup diagnostics)
Include release/versioning guidance for the plugin (semantic versioning and changelog)
Add lightweight static analysis and style checks (ruff/flake8 + mypy or pyright for interface typing)
Verify and document thread-safety/concurrency behavior of shared analyzer instances in MCP service context

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Resilient fallback path worked: after git clone failure, repository content was still processed via zip fallback.
Import-based adapter strategy was feasible (import feasibility 0.97), matching the simple/low-intrusiveness project profile.
MCP plugin artifacts were generated consistently (entrypoint, adapter, service, requirements, README) and service health check passed.
failure_reasons: Primary source acquisition failed due to external network/connectivity error to github.com:443 (curl 28 timeout).
Quality signals are weak/inconsistent because many telemetry fields were defaulted/empty (0 files processed, 0 LOC, 0 endpoint count in one section, empty code review, empty perf metrics).
Original project test validation did not run meaningfully (passed=false with empty details and no test files).
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing cross-source confidence in architectural/code understanding.

node_performance.download_time: Dominant delay source; includes ~75s clone timeout before fallback. Node succeeded functionally via fallback but with degraded reliability.
node_performance.analysis_time: Likely moderate and efficient for a small codebase (10 files), but no per-node timing captured.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; generated standard MCP scaffold quickly relative to total runtime, but instrumentation gaps prevent precise attribution.
node_performance.test_time: Minimal to none; MCP health passed but comprehensive tests were not executed, so test phase was shallow.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: No measured data (reported 0 MB). For this small Python library, expected memory footprint is low-to-moderate; current metric pipeline is insufficient.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: No measured data (reported 0%). Workload likely I/O-bound (network) plus light codegen; actual CPU efficiency cannot be validated.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Repository and generated outputs are small; largest assets are lexicon/text resources. Disk usage is likely efficient, but generated size metrics were not captured.
